title: Myles
content:
{{Top|leg}} {{Otheruses|Myles}} {{GAnom}} {{Character |type=Mandalorian |image=[[File:Myles-OpenSeasons3.png]] |name=Myles |homeworld= |birth= |death=[[44 BBY/Legends|44 BBY]], [[Galidraan/Legends|Galidraan]]''[[The Essential Guide to Warfare]]'' dates the [[Battle of Galidraan]]—including Myles' [[Death/Legends|death]] in [[Jango Fett: Open Seasons 3|''Jango Fett: Open Seasons'' 3]]—to [[44 BBY/Legends|44 BBY]]. |species=A [[Humanoid/Legends|Humanoid]] [[Species/Legends|species]][[Jango Fett: Open Seasons 3|''Jango Fett: Open Seasons'' 3]] |gender= |pronouns=He/him{{CSWECite|II|347|Myles}} |height= |mass= |hair= |feathers= |eyes= |skin= |cyber= |families= |parents= |partners= |siblings= |children= |affiliation=[[True Mandalorians]] |masters= |apprentices= }} {{Quote|Myles! This is Jango! Evac the camp! Evac now!''"
"''Bzzt...Jango–bzzt—repeat?''"
"''Myles, my comlink is out, but if you read me, get out now!|Jango attempting contact Myles|}} '''Myles''' was a [[Mandalorian shock trooper]] and [[Mandalorian Supercommando]] of the [[True Mandalorians]] led by [[Jango Fett/Legends|Jango Fett]]. In [[44 BBY]], he participated in an [[Insurrection on Galidraan|attack]] led by Fett on a [[Galidraanian rebels|rebel group]] on the [[Planet/Legends|world]] of [[Galidraan/Legends|Galidraan]] for the [[governor of Galidraan]]. In exchange, the [[Governor/Legends|governor]] agreed to share information on the True Mandalorian's enemy, [[Tor Vizsla]], leader of the [[Death Watch/Legends|Death Watch]] [[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorian]] splinter group. After defeating the rebels, Myles returned to the Mandalorians' camp while Fett confronted the governor. Fett was then betrayed by the governor and was attacked by Vizsla and his Mandalorian warriors. The governor also reported the True Mandalorians to the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]], falsely claiming they had [[Death/Legends|murdered]] [[Gender/Legends|women]] and [[Youngling/Legends|children]]. When the [[Jedi task force]] arrived, Fett reunited with the True Mandalorians and attacked the [[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-sensitive]] [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knights]]. During the [[Battle of Galidraan|battle]], Myles was slain by a long-[[hair/Legends|haired]] [[Unidentified Jedi (Myles' killer)|Jedi]]. The task force proceeded to kill the rest of Fett's warriors, leaving him as the sole survivor. ==Biography== ===Crushing a rebellion=== {{Quote|Head back to camp.''"
"''What about you?''"
"''I'm going to tell the governor that the insurrection is over...|Jango Fett and Myles after defeating the rebels|}} [[File:GalidraanRebelsDefeated-OpenSeasons3.png|thumb|right|300px|Myles (right) defeated the Galidraanian rebels with the True Mandalorians.]] Myles was a [[Mandalorian Supercommando]]{{InsiderCite|80|The History of the Mandalorians: Exploring the Evolution of a Mighty Race of Warriors}} establishes that members of the [[True Mandalorians]] were [[Mandalorian Supercommando]]s. and [[Mandalorian shock trooper]] who was part of the [[True Mandalorians]], a group loyal to [[Mandalore (title)/Legends|Mandalore]] [[Jaster Mereel]] after the [[Mandalorian/Legends|Mandalorians]] fractured into Mereel's group and the [[Death Watch/Legends|Death Watch]] led by [[Tor Vizsla]][[Jango Fett: Open Seasons 1|''Jango Fett: Open Seasons'' 1]] in [[60 BBY/Legends|60 BBY]].''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' After Mereel was [[Death/Legends|killed]] by Vizsla[[Jango Fett: Open Seasons 2|''Jango Fett: Open Seasons'' 2]] in [[52 BBY/Legends|52 BBY]],[[Jango Fett: Open Seasons 2|''Jango Fett: Open Seasons'' 2]] dates the [[Death/Legends|death]] of [[Jaster Mereel]] to thirty [[Standard year/Legends|years]] before the [[First Battle of Geonosis/Legends|Battle of Geonosis]], which occurred in [[22 BBY/Legends|22 BBY]]. Therefore, Mereel must have died in [[52 BBY/Legends|52 BBY]]. [[Jango Fett/Legends|Jango Fett]] took his place as leader of the True Mandalorians. In [[44 BBY/Legends|44 BBY]], Fett led the True Mandalorians to [[Galidraan/Legends|Galidraan]] in order to perform a job for the [[governor of Galidraan]] in exchange for Vizsla's location. They were tasked with eliminating a [[Galidraanian rebels|rebel group]] that [[Insurrection on Galidraan|threatened]] the [[Governor/Legends|governor's]] reign on the world. The True Mandalorians set up a camp and then traveled to the rebels' location utilizing their [[Jetpack/Legends|jetpacks]]. Arriving at their holdout, the Mandalorian warriors attacked the [[Politics/Legends|political]] activists and swiftly killed them all. Fett requested a report from Myles, to which he informed that all opposition had been neutralized. Jango Fett then ordered Myles and the rest of the True Mandalorians to return to their camp, as he was going to confront the governor about their exchange. Unbeknownst to Fett and his followers, the governor had sided with Vizsla and requested assistance from the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]], informing them that the Mandalorians had slaughtered political activists. Soon after Fett encountered the governor, he was attacked by Vizsla's Death Watch and had his jetpack and communications damaged during his escape. Fett attempted to warn Myles about the incoming [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]], but the shock trooper was met with static and asked Fett to repeat. He attempted a final time to order Myles to evacuate the camp but received no reply. ===Death and legacy=== {{Quote|Mandalorians, I am Master Dooku. You stand accused of Murder. Surrender now and we will ensure that you are treated fairly.|Jedi Master Dooku confronting the Mandalorians|}} [[File:MylesAttacks-OpenSeasons3.jpg|thumb|left|300px|Myles attacks the Jedi before being cut down]] Landing in [[Consular-class light cruiser/Legends|''Consular''-class light cruisers]], the [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knights]] confronted the Mandalorians. The leader of the [[Jedi task force|task force]], [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Dooku/Legends|Dooku]], introduced himself and asked for their surrender, promising fair treatment. Dooku's [[Padawan/Legends|Padawan]]{{DB|character|komarivosa|Vosa, Komari}} [[Komari Vosa]] then spoke up, telling the Mandalorians that if they fought back, that the Jedi would deliver swift justice. Fett commanded his Mandalorians to open fire on the Jedi. Myles and the other [[Soldier/Legends|soldiers]] immediately began [[Battle of Galidraan|attacking]] the Knights. Having their [[Blaster/Legends|blasters]] deflected by the Jedi's [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsabers]], Fett ordered the Mandalorians to switch to projectile-based [[Weapon/Legends|weaponry]] instead. Myles fired a [[Missile/Legends|missile]] from his [[wrist rocket launcher]] alongside the other warriors. The Jedi then attacked the Mandalorians, cutting them down with their [[Sword/Legends|swords]]. Attempting to provide cover for Fett, Myles shot into the air with his jetpack and fired more missiles before a long-[[Hair/Legends|haired]] [[Unidentified Jedi (Myles' killer)|Jedi]] [[Force jump/Legends|leaped]] into the sky and [[Sai tok/Legends|bisected]] the shock trooper, letting his body fall beside Fett. Eventually, the task force had struck down every warrior aside from Fett, who had killed several Jedi in turn, including Myles' killer. Fett then surrendered to the remaining Jedi, who handed him over to the governor of Galidraan, forcing the Mandalorian into [[Slavery/Legends|slavery]]. [[Standard year/Legends|Years]] later in [[32 BBY]], Jango Fett met with Dooku—now known as [[Darth/Legends|Darth]] Tyranus—at the latter's [[Dooku's castle (Kohlma)|castle]] on the [[Moon/Legends|moon]] of [[Kohlma]] to discuss using Fett as the [[Clone template/Legends|genetic template]] for the [[Clone trooper/Legends|clone troopers]] to be created by the [[Kaminoan/Legends|Kaminoan]] [[Cloning/Legends|cloners]]. Fett initially shared distrust in Dooku, having been the leader of the Jedi responsible for the death of his men, including Myles. Eventually, he accepted the former Jedi's offer.[[Jango Fett: Open Seasons 4|''Jango Fett: Open Seasons'' 4]] ==Personality and traits== Myles was a [[Humanoid/Legends|humanoid]] True Mandalorian and follower of Jango Fett. He reported to Fett and followed orders directed to him. ==Skills and abilities== {{Quote|Get airborne. Give me some cover!|Jango Fett commanding Myles to use his jetpack|}} A Mandalorian, he was skilled with a [[Z-6 jetpack/Legends|Z-6 jetpack]] and Mandalorian weaponry for ranged combat. ==Equipment== {{Quote|Chuck your blasters! Switch to projectile weapons!|Jango Fett|}} Myles donned [[Color/Legends|silver]] and blue [[Mandalorian armor/Legends|Mandalorian armor]], distinguishing himself from Fett's red armor and the rest of the True Mandalorian's green armor. His shoulder guards were yellow, and the left guard featured a blue symbol of a [[Mythosaur/Legends|Mythosaur]] [[Bone/Legends|skull]]. He also wielded a [[Blaster rifle/Legends|blaster rifle]] that fired orange [[Laser/Legends|lasers]]. His gauntlet was capable of firing missiles. ==Behind the scenes== Myles first appeared in the [[Jango Fett: Open Seasons 3|third issue]] of the ''[[Star Wars: Jango Fett: Open Seasons]]'' [[Star Wars comic books|comic]] miniseries, written by [[W. Haden Blackman|Haden Blackman]], penciled by [[Ramón F. Bachs]], and published by [[Marvel Comics]] on [[July 31]], [[2002]].{{Marvel|url=comics/issue/54274/star_wars_jango_fett_-_open_seasons_2002_3|text=Star Wars: Jango Fett - Open Seasons (2002) #3}} ==Appearances== *[[Jango Fett: Open Seasons 3|''Jango Fett: Open Seasons'' 3]] {{1st}} {{Flash}} *[[Jango Fett: Open Seasons 4|''Jango Fett: Open Seasons'' 4]] {{Imo}} *''[[Order 66: A Republic Commando Novel]]'' {{Mo}} ==Sources== *''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' ==Notes and references== {{Mediacat|imagecat=Images of Myles}} {{Reflist}} {{Interlang |de=Myles |pl=Myles |pt=Legends:Myles }} [[Category:Individuals of unspecified gender]] [[Category:Individuals of unspecified species]] [[Category:Individuals with he/him pronouns]] [[Category:True Mandalorians]]
